source_jobs.title: Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RAD AI

At Rad AI, we’re on a mission to transform healthcare with artificial intelligence. Founded by a radiologist, our AI-driven solutions are revolutionizing radiology—saving time, reducing burnout, and improving patient care. With one of the largest proprietary radiology report datasets in the world, our AI has helped uncover hundreds of new cancer diagnoses and reduced error rates in tens of millions of radiology reports by nearly 50%.

Rad AI has secured over $140M in funding, including a recently oversubscribed Series C ($68M round) led by Transformation Capital, bringing our valuation to $528M. Our investors include Khosla Ventures, World Innovation Lab, Gradient Ventures, Cone Health Ventures, and others—all backing our mission to empower physicians with cutting-edge AI.

Our latest advancements in generative AI are used by thousands of radiologists daily, supporting more than one-third of radiology groups and healthcare systems and nearly 50% of all medical imaging in the U.S. at partners including Cone Health, Jefferson Einstein Health, Geisinger, Guthrie Healthcare System, and Henry Ford Health.

Recognized as one of the most promising healthcare AI companies by CB Insights and AuntMinnie https://www.radai.com/news/auntminnie-recognizes-rad-ai-omni-reporting-as-2023s-best-new-radiology-software, and ranked by Deloitte https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/technology-media-and-telecommunications/articles/fast500-winners.html as the 19th fastest-growing company in North America, we are building AI-powered solutions that make a real impact. Most recently, Rad AI was named to CNBC’s Disruptor 50 https://www.cnbc.com/2025/06/10/2025-cnbc-disruptor-50-see-the-full-list-of-companies.html list, highlighting the innovation and momentum behind our mission.

If you’re ready to shape the future of healthcare, we’d love to have you on our team!



Why Join Us:

We are seeking a Senior Data Engineer to join our engineering team. With our rapid client growth and ambitious plans for the year ahead, we are expanding the team and looking for a skilled professional to play a key role in building and maintaining data collection systems, pipelines, and management tools.

What You’ll Be Doing:

 - Design and implement the data architecture, ensuring scalability, flexibility, and efficiency using pipeline authoring tools like Metaflow and large-scale data processing technologies like Spark.

 - Define and extend our internal standards for style, maintenance, and best practices for a high-scale data platform.

 - Collaborate with researchers and other stakeholders to understand their data needs including model training and production monitoring systems and develop solutions that meet those requirements.

 - Take ownership of key data engineering projects and work independently to design, develop, and maintain high-quality data solutions.

 - Ensure data quality, integrity, and security by implementing robust data validation, monitoring, and access controls.

 - Evaluate and recommend data technologies and tools to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of the data engineering process.

 - Continuously monitor, maintain, and improve the performance and stability of the data infrastructure.

 

Who We’re Looking For:

 - 5+ years relevant experience in data engineering.

 - Expertise in designing and developing distributed data pipelines using big data technologies on large scale data sets.

 - Deep and hands-on experience designing, planning, productionizing, maintaining and documenting reliable and scalable data infrastructure and data products in complex environments.

 - Solid experience with big data processing and analytics on AWS, using services such as Amazon EMR and AWS Batch.

 - Experience in large scale data processing technologies such as Spark.

 - Expertise in orchestrating workflows using tools like Metaflow.

 - Experience with various database technologies including SQL, NoSQL databases (e.g., AWS DynamoDB, Elasti

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RAD AI

At Rad AI, we’re on a mission to transform healthcare with artificial intelligence. Founded by a radiologist, our AI-driven solutions are revolutionizing radiology—saving time, reducing burnout, and improving patient care. With one of the largest proprietary radiology report datasets in the world, our AI has helped uncover hundreds of new cancer diagnoses and reduced error rates in tens of millions of radiology reports by nearly 50%.

Rad AI has secured over $140M in funding, including a recently oversubscribed Series C ($68M round) led by Transformation Capital, bringing our valuation to $528M. Our investors include Khosla Ventures, World Innovation Lab, Gradient Ventures, Cone Health Ventures, and others—all backing our mission to empower physicians with cutting-edge AI.

Our latest advancements in generative AI are used by thousands of radiologists daily, supporting more than one-third of radiology groups and healthcare systems and nearly 50% of all medical imaging in the U.S. at partners including Cone Health, Jefferson Einstein Health, Geisinger, Guthrie Healthcare System, and Henry Ford Health.

Recognized as one of the most promising healthcare AI companies by CB Insights and AuntMinnie https://www.radai.com/news/auntminnie-recognizes-rad-ai-omni-reporting-as-2023s-best-new-radiology-software, and ranked by Deloitte https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/technology-media-and-telecommunications/articles/fast500-winners.html as the 19th fastest-growing company in North America, we are building AI-powered solutions that make a real impact. Most recently, Rad AI was named to CNBC’s Disruptor 50 https://www.cnbc.com/2025/06/10/2025-cnbc-disruptor-50-see-the-full-list-of-companies.html list, highlighting the innovation and momentum behind our mission.

If you’re ready to shape the future of healthcare, we’d love to have you on our team!



Why Join Us:

The Platform Engineering organization at Rad AI builds the foundations that power all of our products—Reporting, Impressions, and Continuity—and enables product teams to ship reliably, safely, and at scale. Within Platform, the Infrastructure team owns our core cloud infrastructure, platforms, and reliability practices. We’re hiring a Senior Software Engineer to help us design and operate robust, scalable systems. In this role, you’ll contribute to infrastructure architecture, reliability practices, and thoughtful improvements to our workflows. If you’re passionate about building resilient platforms and enjoy collaborating across functions, we’d love to hear from you.

What You'll Be Doing:

 - Architect and evolve our cloud infrastructure (primarily on AWS) across container orchestration (Kubernetes, Elastic Container Service), serverless (e.g., Lambda), virtual machines (e.g., EC2), and data stores to support current and future products.

 - Collaborate with engineering leadership, machine learning, data science, and product partners to help shape our platform vision.

 - Develop and maintain tooling that improves engineering productivity and developer experience.

 - Promote sustainable incident response and lead blameless post-incident reviews.

 - Manage network and systems monitoring, design alert strategies, and participate in an equitable on‑call rotation.

Who We're Looking For:

 - A data‑informed approach and a track record of effective problem solving.

 - Bring 6+ years of hands-on infrastructure / platform development experience (or equivalent practical experience) in modern, cloud-native environments, with a track record of owning critical systems in production.

 - Extensive experience operating Kubernetes in production, including familiarity with cluster lifecycle management, scaling, and container security.

 - Proficiency in one or more languages—Python (preferred) and/or Bash—and familiarity with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools such as CDK, Terraform or Pulumi.

 - Strong familiarity with AWS and/or GCP services.

 - Networking fundamentals

source_jobs.title: Director of Engineering, Reporting Product
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RAD AI

At Rad AI, we’re on a mission to transform healthcare with artificial intelligence. Founded by a radiologist, our AI-driven solutions are revolutionizing radiology—saving time, reducing burnout, and improving patient care. With one of the largest proprietary radiology report datasets in the world, our AI has helped uncover hundreds of new cancer diagnoses and reduced error rates in tens of millions of radiology reports by nearly 50%.

Rad AI has secured over $140M in funding, including a recently oversubscribed Series C ($68M round) led by Transformation Capital, bringing our valuation to $528M. Our investors include Khosla Ventures, World Innovation Lab, Gradient Ventures, Cone Health Ventures, and others—all backing our mission to empower physicians with cutting-edge AI.

Our latest advancements in generative AI are used by thousands of radiologists daily, supporting more than one-third of radiology groups and healthcare systems and nearly 50% of all medical imaging in the U.S. at partners including Cone Health, Jefferson Einstein Health, Geisinger, Guthrie Healthcare System, and Henry Ford Health.

Recognized as one of the most promising healthcare AI companies by CB Insights and AuntMinnie https://www.radai.com/news/auntminnie-recognizes-rad-ai-omni-reporting-as-2023s-best-new-radiology-software, and ranked by Deloitte https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/technology-media-and-telecommunications/articles/fast500-winners.html as the 19th fastest-growing company in North America, we are building AI-powered solutions that make a real impact. Most recently, Rad AI was named to CNBC’s Disruptor 50 https://www.cnbc.com/2025/06/10/2025-cnbc-disruptor-50-see-the-full-list-of-companies.html list, highlighting the innovation and momentum behind our mission.

If you’re ready to shape the future of healthcare, we’d love to have you on our team!



Rad AI is on a mission to make radiologists' lives better and patient care smarter. Our AI-powered software helps radiology groups work more efficiently, communicate more clearly, and deliver more consistent, higher-quality reports. We're a team of people who care deeply about what we build and about the clinicians and patients whose lives depend on it. We'd love to have you join us.




WHY WE NEED YOU

A radiology report is the moment the entire diagnostic process culminates. It's where a radiologist's expertise becomes actionable insight for the ordering physician, for the care team, for the patient. At Rad AI, our Reporting product is the heart of what we do. It's where our AI meets the real world.

The engineering team behind that product doesn't just ship features, they shape what radiologists experience every single day. When that experience is excellent, we earn trust, and trust is everything in healthcare. When it falls short, it's not just a bug or a missed deadline; it's a radiologist losing faith in AI at the exact moment they needed it most.

That's why we need you. You're the person who holds the standard and ensures that the team building our most important product is doing the best work of their careers. Your leadership is what turns a group of talented engineers into a cohesive force that ships software radiologists love. Without someone exceptional in this role, we can't build the Reporting product our customers deserve. And without that product, Rad AI doesn't win.




HERE'S WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

You'll lead the engineering team responsible for our Reporting product — the flagship of Rad AI's platform. That means owning the technical strategy for the product, partnering closely with Product and Design to shape the roadmap, and making sure your team has everything they need to build, ship, and iterate with confidence. You'll be equally at home diving into an architectural decision and coaching an engineering manager through a hard conversation.

This is a leadership role, but you're not removed from the work; you're in it. You'll set the technical bar, model

source_jobs.title: Staff ML Research Scientist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RAD AI

At Rad AI, we’re on a mission to transform healthcare with artificial intelligence. Founded by a radiologist, our AI-driven solutions are revolutionizing radiology—saving time, reducing burnout, and improving patient care. With one of the largest proprietary radiology report datasets in the world, our AI has helped uncover hundreds of new cancer diagnoses and reduced error rates in tens of millions of radiology reports by nearly 50%.

Rad AI has secured over $140M in funding, including a recently oversubscribed Series C ($68M round) led by Transformation Capital, bringing our valuation to $528M. Our investors include Khosla Ventures, World Innovation Lab, Gradient Ventures, Cone Health Ventures, and others—all backing our mission to empower physicians with cutting-edge AI.

Our latest advancements in generative AI are used by thousands of radiologists daily, supporting more than one-third of radiology groups and healthcare systems and nearly 50% of all medical imaging in the U.S. at partners including Cone Health, Jefferson Einstein Health, Geisinger, Guthrie Healthcare System, and Henry Ford Health.

Recognized as one of the most promising healthcare AI companies by CB Insights and AuntMinnie https://www.radai.com/news/auntminnie-recognizes-rad-ai-omni-reporting-as-2023s-best-new-radiology-software, and ranked by Deloitte https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/technology-media-and-telecommunications/articles/fast500-winners.html as the 19th fastest-growing company in North America, we are building AI-powered solutions that make a real impact. Most recently, Rad AI was named to CNBC’s Disruptor 50 https://www.cnbc.com/2025/06/10/2025-cnbc-disruptor-50-see-the-full-list-of-companies.html list, highlighting the innovation and momentum behind our mission.

If you’re ready to shape the future of healthcare, we’d love to have you on our team!



Why Join Us?

We're looking for a Staff Machine Learning Research Scientist to help define and drive Rad AI's next generation of applied research in NLP and clinical AI.

We work across LLMs, retrieval, representation learning, speech and multimodal modeling, and we care as much about evaluation and reliability as we do about state-of-the-art results. You will have scope, ownership, and a direct line from research to product.

You'll collaborate closely with clinicians, engineers, and product leaders to translate foundational research into production-scale systems that improve outcomes for doctors and patients alike. As we grow, you will help shape standards for model quality, safety, and observability, and contribute to strategic initiatives that include computer vision and vision-language work.



What You'll Do:

 - Own end-to-end applied research: frame the problem, design experiments, ship to production, and monitor impact against real-world metrics.

 - Set technical direction across LLMs, retrieval, and multimodal; run ablations/error analysis that change product decisions.

 - Build evaluation that matters: link offline metrics to online outcomes; define thresholds, monitoring, and rollback.

 - Partner to deliver with engineering and product—and, when relevant, clinicians/domain experts—to align data, success criteria, and timelines.

 - Raise the bar by mentoring peers and codifying standards for reliability, safety, and documentation.

 - Improve the platform (data, training, serving, observability) to speed iteration and ensure reproducibility.

 - Explore new directions, with computer vision/vision-language work as a nice-to-have for future strategic initiatives.



What We're Looking For:

 - MS or PhD (or equivalent research experience) in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Computational Linguistics, Biomedical Informatics, or related quantitative field.

 - 7+ years of applied ML research experience (or PhD + 5 years, or equivalent evidence of Staff-level impact).

 - Depth in one or more areas: LLMs and NLP, computer vision, speech, recommendation/rankin

source_jobs.title: Market Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RAD AI

At Rad AI, we’re on a mission to transform healthcare with artificial intelligence. Founded by a radiologist, our AI-driven solutions are revolutionizing radiology—saving time, reducing burnout, and improving patient care. With one of the largest proprietary radiology report datasets in the world, our AI has helped uncover hundreds of new cancer diagnoses and reduced error rates in tens of millions of radiology reports by nearly 50%.

Rad AI has secured over $140M in funding, including a recently oversubscribed Series C ($68M round) led by Transformation Capital, bringing our valuation to $528M. Our investors include Khosla Ventures, World Innovation Lab, Gradient Ventures, Cone Health Ventures, and others—all backing our mission to empower physicians with cutting-edge AI.

Our latest advancements in generative AI are used by thousands of radiologists daily, supporting more than one-third of radiology groups and healthcare systems and nearly 50% of all medical imaging in the U.S. at partners including Cone Health, Jefferson Einstein Health, Geisinger, Guthrie Healthcare System, and Henry Ford Health.

Recognized as one of the most promising healthcare AI companies by CB Insights and AuntMinnie https://www.radai.com/news/auntminnie-recognizes-rad-ai-omni-reporting-as-2023s-best-new-radiology-software, and ranked by Deloitte https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/technology-media-and-telecommunications/articles/fast500-winners.html as the 19th fastest-growing company in North America, we are building AI-powered solutions that make a real impact. Most recently, Rad AI was named to CNBC’s Disruptor 50 https://www.cnbc.com/2025/06/10/2025-cnbc-disruptor-50-see-the-full-list-of-companies.html list, highlighting the innovation and momentum behind our mission.

If you’re ready to shape the future of healthcare, we’d love to have you on our team!



As a Market Development Representative (MDR) at Rad AI, you'll be on the front lines of our growth engine — converting interest into qualified pipeline and high-quality meetings for our Sales team. You'll engage radiology groups and health systems, educating prospects on Rad AI's solutions and creating meaningful entry points for our reps.

This is an individual contributor role with clear meeting targets, structured outbound prospecting, and inbound follow-up ownership. You'll serve as a key connector between Marketing, Sales, and RevOps — and while you won't be building the function from scratch, you'll have real influence over our playbooks, messaging, and best practices as we scale.

What You’ll Do

 - Manage and qualify inbound leads from marketing campaigns, website forms, events, and partner channels

 - Conduct personalized outbound outreach (email, LinkedIn, phone) to targeted accounts to generate new pipeline

 - Research target accounts — radiology practices, health systems, and academic centers — to map org structure and identify the right stakeholders

 - Tailor outreach based on persona (clinical, economic, strategic, technical), segment, and likely use case

 - Master the Rad AI story and clearly articulate our value to radiologists, IT leaders, and executive buyers

 - Partner closely with Account Executives to build qualified pipeline and hand off meetings that align to agreed-upon SLAs

 - Document all activity, notes, and lead status accurately and consistently in HubSpot

 - Execute and refine sequenced cadences across key segments (private practice, community systems, academic centers, IDNs)

 - Share feedback on messaging, ICP fit, objections, and lead quality to continuously improve Marketing and Sales alignment

 - Meet and exceed core MDR metrics: activities, meetings set, show rates, and qualified pipeline created

What We're Looking For

 - 2+ years of experience in a Sales, Sales Development, or Business Development role (B2B SaaS preferred)

 - Experience engaging senior stakeholders — clinical leaders, CIOs, CFOs — in complex technical sales enviro

source_jobs.title: Senior Implementation Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RAD AI

At Rad AI, we’re on a mission to transform healthcare with artificial intelligence. Founded by a radiologist, our AI-driven solutions are revolutionizing radiology—saving time, reducing burnout, and improving patient care. With one of the largest proprietary radiology report datasets in the world, our AI has helped uncover hundreds of new cancer diagnoses and reduced error rates in tens of millions of radiology reports by nearly 50%.

Rad AI has secured over $140M in funding, including a recently oversubscribed Series C ($68M round) led by Transformation Capital, bringing our valuation to $528M. Our investors include Khosla Ventures, World Innovation Lab, Gradient Ventures, Cone Health Ventures, and others—all backing our mission to empower physicians with cutting-edge AI.

Our latest advancements in generative AI are used by thousands of radiologists daily, supporting more than one-third of radiology groups and healthcare systems and nearly 50% of all medical imaging in the U.S. at partners including Cone Health, Jefferson Einstein Health, Geisinger, Guthrie Healthcare System, and Henry Ford Health.

Recognized as one of the most promising healthcare AI companies by CB Insights and AuntMinnie https://www.radai.com/news/auntminnie-recognizes-rad-ai-omni-reporting-as-2023s-best-new-radiology-software, and ranked by Deloitte https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/technology-media-and-telecommunications/articles/fast500-winners.html as the 19th fastest-growing company in North America, we are building AI-powered solutions that make a real impact. Most recently, Rad AI was named to CNBC’s Disruptor 50 https://www.cnbc.com/2025/06/10/2025-cnbc-disruptor-50-see-the-full-list-of-companies.html list, highlighting the innovation and momentum behind our mission.

If you’re ready to shape the future of healthcare, we’d love to have you on our team!



Why Join Us:

At Rad AI, we combine world-class radiology and AI expertise to build products that maximize radiologist productivity, expand access to high-quality care, and improve patient outcomes. Join us and help transform how radiology is practiced for patients and health systems around the world.

We’re looking for a Senior Implementation Engineer with extensive experience with interfaces to clinical data systems. Rad AI’s award-winning software has experienced tremendous market growth. We are looking for a teammate with a “can do'' attitude that is adaptable in a fast-paced, high-growth team.

What You’ll Be Doing:

 - Develop and maintain HL7 and FHIR interfaces with our customers' systems, working closely with the customer's interface teams.

 - Lead the standardization of our interfaces across products and the creation of documentation for our interfaces.

 - Provide technical expertise, guidance, and support during product implementation to ensure successful customer utilization.

 - Work on monitoring and alerting of interface health.

 - Test and troubleshoot HL7 and FHIR implementations to ensure reliability and compatibility.

 - Facilitate end-to-end implementation testing with customers.

 - Provide end-to-end technical assistance for our new clients during their implementation testing process.

 - Collaborate with Developers and Engineers on interface-related tasks to continually improve integration methods and/or custom functionality to meet customer needs.

 - Provide advanced technical customer support as necessary after go-live.

 - Assist in mentoring and upskilling teammates in HL7 and FHIR, and acting as an internal leader in FHIR and HL7 best practices.

Who We’re Looking For:

 - 5+ years of experience in HL7 v2 interfacing.

 - Proven technical background, should be comfortable reading logs, source code, querying DBs using SQL.

 - Familiarity with REST APIs, JSON, and XML.

 - Familiarity/Experience with JavaScript, Typescript, and/or Python.

 - Strong understanding of the Healthcare IT ecosystem and major clinical and system integration points.

 -

Why Join Us:

At Rad AI, we combine world-class radiology and AI expertise to build products that maximize radiologist productivity, expand access to high-quality care, and improve patient outcomes. Join us and help transform how radiology is practiced for patients and health systems around the world.

We are looking for an Implementation Project Manager with deep experience leading software implementation projects. As Rad AI’s award-winning platform continues its rapid adoption, you will play a key role in guiding customers through successful deployments. You bring a proactive, “can do” mindset and thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment where adaptability and collaboration are essential.

What You’ll Be Doing:

 - Work independently to plan, monitor, and manage multiple complex customer-facing projects

 - Lead the end-to-end delivery of Rad AI’s products to radiology practices and health systems, from kick-off to go-live and transition to support

 - Facilitate Discovery sessions to understand client workflows, requirements, and success criteria

 - Develop detailed project plans, including timelines, milestones, and resource needs

 - Coordinate cross-functional teams, including Product, Engineering, and Integration resources, to meet project objectives and deadlines

 - Serve as the primary client-facing contact throughout the implementation

 - Ensure a smooth onboarding process by managing project timelines, dependencies, and communications with executive, clinical, and IT stakeholders

 - Track and manage feature requests, ensuring clear communication, documentation, and prioritization

 - Monitor project health, proactively identify risks, and escalate blockers, as needed

 - Issue resolution and mitigation strategies based on data-driven decision making

 - Conduct weekly status meetings with internal and external stakeholders

 - Collaborate and coordinate with Rad AI’s product, engineering, and customer success teams for project goal setting, data integration, testing, and product feedback

 - Provide ongoing updates to leadership throughout the project, via

source_jobs.title: Senior Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RAD AI

At Rad AI, we’re on a mission to transform healthcare with artificial intelligence. Founded by a radiologist, our AI-driven solutions are revolutionizing radiology—saving time, reducing burnout, and improving patient care. With one of the largest proprietary radiology report datasets in the world, our AI has helped uncover hundreds of new cancer diagnoses and reduced error rates in tens of millions of radiology reports by nearly 50%.

Rad AI has secured over $140M in funding, including a recently oversubscribed Series C ($68M round) led by Transformation Capital, bringing our valuation to $528M. Our investors include Khosla Ventures, World Innovation Lab, Gradient Ventures, Cone Health Ventures, and others—all backing our mission to empower physicians with cutting-edge AI.

Our latest advancements in generative AI are used by thousands of radiologists daily, supporting more than one-third of radiology groups and healthcare systems and nearly 50% of all medical imaging in the U.S. at partners including Cone Health, Jefferson Einstein Health, Geisinger, Guthrie Healthcare System, and Henry Ford Health.

Recognized as one of the most promising healthcare AI companies by CB Insights and AuntMinnie https://www.radai.com/news/auntminnie-recognizes-rad-ai-omni-reporting-as-2023s-best-new-radiology-software, and ranked by Deloitte https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/technology-media-and-telecommunications/articles/fast500-winners.html as the 19th fastest-growing company in North America, we are building AI-powered solutions that make a real impact. Most recently, Rad AI was named to CNBC’s Disruptor 50 https://www.cnbc.com/2025/06/10/2025-cnbc-disruptor-50-see-the-full-list-of-companies.html list, highlighting the innovation and momentum behind our mission.

If you’re ready to shape the future of healthcare, we’d love to have you on our team!



Why We Need You

The products we build are used every day by radiologists making critical decisions with diagnoses that determine what care patients receive next. That’s not a context where “good enough” ships.

We’re looking for a Senior Engineering Manager who can hold that standard while also scaling a team that keeps pace with Rad AI’s growth. Someone who has lived through the full lifecycle of production software from the architectural decision that aged well to the one that didn’t and who brings that hard-won judgment to every conversation with their engineers.

This isn’t a coordination role. You’ll shape technical direction, develop the engineers around you, and drive outcomes on one of our core products. The team you build here will define how clinicians experience AI for years to come.



Here’s What You’ll Be Doing

You’ll lead one of our core product engineering teams where you’ll:

 - Own end-to-end execution on your team’s roadmap from planning, prioritization, delivery, rollout, and iteration across one or more core products.

 - Raise the technical bar by guiding architecture and design decisions, ensuring your team builds observable, maintainable systems that meet healthcare-grade reliability standards.

 - Partner cross-functionally with Product, Implementations, Design, Clinical, Customer Success, and GTM counterparts to translate customer problems into clear, actionable engineering work.

 - Drive continuous improvement across planning, estimation, code review, incident response, on-call, and documentation while evolving team processes and pushing the limits of AI to unlock new levels of efficiency and quality.

 - Use data and metrics to drive decisions, defining and tracking the right leading indicators for reliability, performance, and delivery health.

 - Hire exceptional engineers, develop the talent already on your team, and foster a culture of psychological safety, ownership, and continuous learning.

Who We’re Looking For

 - 6–8+ years of experience managing software engineers with a proven track record leading teams that ship and operate production

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, SDK
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RAD AI

At Rad AI, we’re on a mission to transform healthcare with artificial intelligence. Founded by a radiologist, our AI-driven solutions are revolutionizing radiology—saving time, reducing burnout, and improving patient care. With one of the largest proprietary radiology report datasets in the world, our AI has helped uncover hundreds of new cancer diagnoses and reduced error rates in tens of millions of radiology reports by nearly 50%.

Rad AI has secured over $140M in funding, including a recently oversubscribed Series C ($68M round) led by Transformation Capital, bringing our valuation to $528M. Our investors include Khosla Ventures, World Innovation Lab, Gradient Ventures, Cone Health Ventures, and others—all backing our mission to empower physicians with cutting-edge AI.

Our latest advancements in generative AI are used by thousands of radiologists daily, supporting more than one-third of radiology groups and healthcare systems and nearly 50% of all medical imaging in the U.S. at partners including Cone Health, Jefferson Einstein Health, Geisinger, Guthrie Healthcare System, and Henry Ford Health.

Recognized as one of the most promising healthcare AI companies by CB Insights and AuntMinnie https://www.radai.com/news/auntminnie-recognizes-rad-ai-omni-reporting-as-2023s-best-new-radiology-software, and ranked by Deloitte https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/technology-media-and-telecommunications/articles/fast500-winners.html as the 19th fastest-growing company in North America, we are building AI-powered solutions that make a real impact. Most recently, Rad AI was named to CNBC’s Disruptor 50 https://www.cnbc.com/2025/06/10/2025-cnbc-disruptor-50-see-the-full-list-of-companies.html list, highlighting the innovation and momentum behind our mission.

If you’re ready to shape the future of healthcare, we’d love to have you on our team!




ABOUT THE SDK AND DICOM ROUTING

DICOM is the standard for moving medical images across the imaging stack—between modalities, PACS, viewing workstations, AI services, and downstream reporting systems. How well that flow is orchestrated determines whether AI can be deployed reliably at scale: which studies get routed where, how outputs come back, and how everything stays in sync with the radiologist's workflow. DICOM routing is the plumbing that makes the rest of the platform possible.

The Rad AI SDK is how partners across the imaging ecosystem—AI vendors, modality and PACS providers, reporting and workflow tools, and downstream care systems—plug into that platform. It is the surface where partner-generated content (preliminary reports, findings, measurements, worklist signals, and more) flows into the radiologist's reporting experience, and where partners can consume the routing, orchestration, and reporting capabilities Rad AI exposes. A well-designed SDK turns Rad AI Orchestration from an internal platform into an open ecosystem.

Together, DICOM routing and the SDK form the connective layer that makes Rad AI's broader platform real: routing standardizes how images and AI outputs move, and the SDK standardizes how partners build on top of it.



THE ROLE

This is a high-priority role with tight timelines. We need a Product Manager who can move quickly, work cross-functionally, and connect technical execution with a broader commercial and platform strategy.

You will own product planning and execution for the Rad AI SDK and the DICOM routing capabilities it exposes to partners. You'll define the developer experience, the integration patterns, and the routing workflows that let ecosystem partners build on Rad AI's platform. You'll partner deeply with engineering on the technical foundations, and with leadership on how this work supports a credible RSNA story and a broader platform narrative.




HERE'S WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING:

 - Own the roadmap and execution for the Rad AI SDK and DICOM routing product initiatives, end-to-end.

 - Define the developer experience for partners b

Rad AI's engineering and product organizations are growing quickly behind our award-winning product. You'll elevate your career in a mission-driven, high-growth environment where generative AI is transforming radiology workflows to save physicians time, reduce burnout, and improve patient care.

In this role you'll own program delivery for Rad AI Reporting, the product line that powers real-world radiology reporting, streamlines workflows, and reduces cognitive load for clinicians. You'll sit within Product Management and split your time between driving the reporting product roadmap with engineering and leading the client integration and deployment programs that get Reporting live inside complex health-system environments. That means owning integration go-lives end to end and being technical enough to roll up your sleeves when an HL7, FHIR, or PACS integration misbehaves.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING


NEW CLIENT INTEGRATION

 - Align delivery of new vendor integrations at scale by coordinating with deployment resources and engineering resources to align delivery of new vendor integrations at scale.

 - Serve as the escalation and coordination point for integration issues during deployment, triaging and diagnosing HL7, FHIR, DICOM, and PACS/RIS/EHR integration problems and driving them to resolution with engineering, implementations, and customer teams.

 - Coordinate security reviews, BAAs/data-use agreements for new integration partners

 - Build repeatable playbooks, runbooks, and KPI dashboards that make integrations faster, more predictable, and measurable. This includes tracking key KPIs like time-to-go-live, defect rates, and adoption.


REPORTING PRODUCT DELIVERY

 - Drive cross-functional execution of roadmap initiatives across the Reporting product line, partnering closely with engineering, design, implementations, and clinical teams.

 - Manage program schedules, scope, and dependencies by identifying risks early and developing mitigation strategies to keep delivery on track.

 - Own release coordination, covering QA, testing sign-off, and stakeholder readiness before eac

WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

You’ll join a small team of full-cycle recruiters who support hiring across all functions including implementations, support, customer success, product management and engineering roles. You will identify and engage the right people who will shape the future of healthcare AI. You will not only drive our recruitment efforts but also help cultivate an inclusive, transparent, and collaborative culture where talent thrives.

As we continue to grow, our recruiting team is critical in attracting and retaining the exceptional talent we need to achieve our ambitious goals. You will play a key role in shaping the future of Rad AI by recruiting across various functions— from engineering and product to operations and customer success.

source_jobs.title: Product Lead, Orchestration
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT RAD AI

At Rad AI, we’re on a mission to transform healthcare with artificial intelligence. Founded by a radiologist, our AI-driven solutions are revolutionizing radiology—saving time, reducing burnout, and improving patient care. With one of the largest proprietary radiology report datasets in the world, our AI has helped uncover hundreds of new cancer diagnoses and reduced error rates in tens of millions of radiology reports by nearly 50%.

Rad AI has secured over $140M in funding, including a recently oversubscribed Series C ($68M round) led by Transformation Capital, bringing our valuation to $528M. Our investors include Khosla Ventures, World Innovation Lab, Gradient Ventures, Cone Health Ventures, and others—all backing our mission to empower physicians with cutting-edge AI.

Our latest advancements in generative AI are used by thousands of radiologists daily, supporting more than one-third of radiology groups and healthcare systems and nearly 50% of all medical imaging in the U.S. at partners including Cone Health, Jefferson Einstein Health, Geisinger, Guthrie Healthcare System, and Henry Ford Health.

Recognized as one of the most promising healthcare AI companies by CB Insights and AuntMinnie https://www.radai.com/news/auntminnie-recognizes-rad-ai-omni-reporting-as-2023s-best-new-radiology-software, and ranked by Deloitte https://www2.deloitte.com/us/en/pages/technology-media-and-telecommunications/articles/fast500-winners.html as the 19th fastest-growing company in North America, we are building AI-powered solutions that make a real impact. Most recently, Rad AI was named to CNBC’s Disruptor 50 https://www.cnbc.com/2025/06/10/2025-cnbc-disruptor-50-see-the-full-list-of-companies.html list, highlighting the innovation and momentum behind our mission.

If you’re ready to shape the future of healthcare, we’d love to have you on our team!



We build AI that sits directly in the radiologist’s workflow:

 - Rad AI Impressions automatically drafts the impression section of reports in real time.

 - Rad AI Reporting is a full radiology reporting platform powered by GenAI.

 - Rad AI Continuity is our patient follow-up coordination platform, identifying actionable findings in radiology reports and closing the loop between radiology and downstream care.

 - Rad AI Orchestration is our emerging platform layer for workflow, interoperability, and intelligent routing across the imaging stack—connecting modalities, PACS, AI partners, and downstream systems into a single, coherent operating fabric.

Orchestration is one of the most important strategic bets at Rad AI. It is the connective tissue that turns point AI capabilities into a true imaging platform—and it sits at the center of how health systems will buy, deploy, and scale AI in radiology over the next decade. We now need a senior leader to take it from concept to a credible, market-ready product and business.



HERE’S WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING:

You will operate as the product and business owner of Orchestration within Rad AI. You own the vision, the roadmap, the commercial strategy, and the day-to-day execution that connects them. You’ll design and run a focused, cross-functional team with clear goals, decision rights, and accountability—a senior operator, not a narrow PM.

You’ll be deeply embedded with customers, partners, and your team in equal measure. You translate health system and imaging realities into product priorities, turn engineering capacity into shipped capability, and make sure each launch lands as a real commercial moment—not just a release. You’ll work closely with the CPO, CEO, and broader executive team, but you won’t wait for permission to act.

In particular, you will:

 - Own Orchestration end-to-end – lead strategy from concept through launch and commercialization, and be accountable for adoption, revenue, and platform impact.

 - Define the platform narrative – articulate how Orchestration fits into the broader Rad AI platform and the market stor

If you’re ready to shape the future of healthcare, we’d love to have you on our team!



We build AI that sits directly in the radiologist’s workflow:

 - Rad AI Impressions automatically drafts the impression section of reports in real time.

 - Rad AI Reporting is a full radiology reporting platform powered by GenAI.

 - Rad AI Continuity is our patient follow‑up coordination platform, identifying actionable findings in radiology reports and closing the loop between radiology and downstream care.

Continuity is already delivering real clinical and financial impact for health systems, improving follow‑up completion rates and catching cancers and other serious conditions earlier. Now we need someone to turn this proven product into a scaled, standalone business inside Rad AI.



WHY WE NEED YOU:

Continuity has crossed the line from promising experiment to real business and we are looking to add a strong Head of Product to oversee a cross-functional team. It has established recurring revenue, a strong pipeline, lighthouse health system customers, and clear proof that it changes patient outcomes and hospital economics. But today, its future still depends on how well we can focus, prioritize, and execute.

Without you, Continuity risks being “just another product line” inside a busy, fast‑growing company. Roadmaps get reshuffled. Implementations take longer than they should. Customers feel the friction of a team that’s splitting its attention. Most importantly, patients who should get follow‑up care never do.

You change that trajectory.

You treat Continuity like your own startup inside Rad AI — with a clear vision, dedicated team, and simple, rigorous operating model. You connect product, engineering, clinical, implementations, and go‑to‑market into a single, coherent business. You protect focus, make hard tradeoffs, and turn great technology into reliable, referenceable outcomes for some of the largest health systems in the country.

If you do your job well, Continuity becomes the way health systems close the loop on radiology findings at scale — and a core pillar of Rad AI’s long‑term success. If we don’t hire you, we risk leaving that opportunity, and those pati

notes: High-tech startup with strong AI focus, multiple engineering roles, and recent funding
